case_title: 200 PAUL, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. UNITEDLAYER, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY

docket_entries.description: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT AND EXHIBITS THERETO HEARING SET FOR MAR-28-2023 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 501

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAR-28-2023 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF 200 PAUL, LLC'S MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT AND EXHIBITS THERETO, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MATTER IS TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD IN DEPARTMENT 501 ON THE SAME DATE AT THE SAME TIME. THAT DEPARTMENT HEARS ALL REAL PROPERTY/EJECTMENT ACTIONS PER SF LOCAL RULE 8.10A1. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY PROCEEDINGS IS DENIED. THERE IS NO BINDING ARBITRATION CLAUSE COVERING THIS DISPUTE. WHILE SECTION 3.3.1 REFERENCES "MEDIATION OR ARBITRATION BEFORE RESORTING TO THE FILING OF SUIT," THERE IS NO REQUIREMENT TO PROCEED WITH THE DISPUTES IN ARBITRATION ONLY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jul-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Brian J. Wagner; Kutak Rock LLP; 949-417-0999; appearing as counsel for defendant Unitedlayer, LLC.
Karl Runft; 415-399-2900; appearing as counsel for plaintiff 200 Paul, LLC.

Defendant contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY PROCEEDINGS is DENIED. There is no binding arbitration clause covering this dispute. While Section 3.3.1 references "mediation or arbitration before resorting to the filing of suit," there is no requirement to proceed with the disputes in arbitration only.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Karl Runft, 415.399.2900 of Peterson Martin Reynolds specially appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff 200 Paul, LLC.
No other appearances.

Discussed. Mr. Runft ask the Court clarifying questions regarding the tentative ruling. The Court states that more details are needed explaining why each item should be redacted. The Court further states that according to the law it is in the public interest to redact only what is absolutely necessary.

PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO FILE UNDER SEAL PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT AND EXHIBITS THERETO is DENIED without prejudice. Declaration in support of the motion is conclusory and does not support the request as to all portions of the complaint sought to be sealed. Plaintiff may re-file this motion with competent evidence addressing each item listed in the notice of motion.
